zotero.abstractNote: We propose a new method to analyze multi-subject neuroimaging data, such as fMRI recordings. Our approach, MultiView ICA, assumes that each subject's data is a linear mixture of some shared components and subject-specific noise. We show that under mild conditions, the shared components can be recovered by maximizing the independence of the estimated sources across subjects. We provide a fast algorithm to solve this problem, and demonstrate its effectiveness on both synthetic and real fMRI data, where it outperforms standard methods like Group ICA or Procrustes-based alignment.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 33 (NeurIPS 2020)

extra.Editors: H. Larochelle and M. Ranzato and R. Hadsell and M. F. Balcan and H. Lin

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 33 (NeurIPS 2020)
